The rates are as below. They may be varied at any time. Charges are non-refundable, only be paid in cash . Credit cards and other forms of payment are not accepted. Whenever possible the cashier should be presented with the exact money.
The current Consular rate of exchange  will apply.

[Table:  Sports, Scientific Or Cultural Activity; Official Exchange Programs]
Service charge applicable when applying at the Austria Visa Application Centre:

[Service charge]

Please Note:
- You will be required to pay the VFS service fee online. Please keep your international credit card/debit card handy while booking an appointment.
- Service charges are inclusive of VAT levied per application over and above the visa fees.
Optional value added services
To add value to our customer, we offer  some value added service. You will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the  visa application fee when you attend the visa service centre only. To know  about these services please refer the additional service page of this website.
Note: Please note that you may be charged visa fee even  though you meet the above information. This will be done only in consultation  with the Embassy of Austria.
visa_types.processing_time:
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to apply for a visa 1 month in advance to the travel dates (due to the long delivery dates, which are not within the Embassy’s influence and take sometimes more than 1 week for 1 direction). 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
visa_types.forms:
This page hosts the form required for  your Visa  Application . Please click on the relevant form to download your visa  application form.

If you can’t prepare the Visa  Application form by yourself, our manager can fill  the Visa application form for you.
Note 
 Minorities (before 16) must have 2  signatures in application form from relatives: mother and father.
 Minorities (after 16) must have 3  signatures in application form from relatives: child, mother and father.
Application form filling
 Dear applicants, kindly be informed,  that our consultants of Service Visa Center of Austria can fill in  your visa application form.
visa_types.overview: When applying for a visa, you are  required to bring 2 photographs as part of completing and submitting your visa  application.
visa_types.full_text:
Overview
If your travel to Austria is for one of the following purpose, you are eligible to apply for this visa.
- Participants in scientific, cultural and artistic activities:
- Language course (up to three months)
- Participants in official twin city programs:
- Pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers:
- Start of academic studies as scholarship student (up to three months)
- Student exchange (up to three months)
- Youth exchange programs
- Workers posted to Austria for specific short-term activities
- Participants in international sports events and accompanying persons in their professional capacity
Description
If you plan to go to Austria to visit relatives for a period not to exceed 90 days and your country is not on the visa-free list, you need a Schengen travel visa (Visa C).
Please note that in order to apply for a travel visa at the Austrian Embassy, your only destination or your main destination (longest duration of stay) must be Austria. In the absence of a main destination (e.g. same duration of stay in two or more countries) you can apply at any of these countries but it is recommended that you apply at the country of first entry because your passport will show the entry stamp of your first destination only.
The applicant should send the invitation of the Austrian University to VFS by e-mail.
Participants at events organized by International Organizations based in Vienna, should send an e-mail to VFS with the according invitation.
Contact us by email: info.auteth@vfshelpline.com (mailto:info.auteth@vfshelpline.com)
Main Rules:
- Submission process can be strictly realized by personal presence if the applicant must provide biometric data.
- Austria Service Visa Center doesn’t accept documents form third persons by letter of attorney or corporative attorney.
Note
Before submission kindly check if your dates in Austria are longer than to other countries of Schengen area.
All documents that are not in English or German must be translated with a certified translation: seal and signature.
Please, check the quality and fullness of the translated documents as any incompliance is the applicant’s responsibility when submitting the documents.
If a family with minors wants to apply, please, book firstly for the adults.
Please be informed that zero balance statements are not of use for the visa processing process.

## Visa Fees
The rates are as below. They may be varied at any time. Charges are non-refundable, only be paid in cash . Credit cards and other forms of payment are not accepted. Whenever possible the cashier should be presented with the exact money.
The current Consular rate of exchange  will apply.

[Table:  Sports, Scientific Or Cultural Activity; Official Exchange Programs]
Service charge applicable when applying at the Austria Visa Application Centre:

[Service charge]

Please Note:
- You will be required to pay the VFS service fee online. Please keep your international credit card/debit card handy while booking an appointment.
- Service charges are inclusive of VAT levied per application over and above the visa fees.
Optional value added services
To add value to our customer, we offer  some value added service. You will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the  visa application fee when you attend the visa service centre only. To know  about these services please refer the additional service page of this website.
Note: Please note that you may be charged visa fee even  though you meet the above information. This will be done only in consultation  with the Embassy of Austria.

## Documents Required
Click here to download the list of documents required
Kindly note:
Personal appearance of applicant is required.
All documents must be submitted in original as well as in a clear photocopy at the time of making an application.

## Photo Specifications

## Overview

 When applying for a visa, you are  required to bring 2 photographs as part of completing and submitting your visa  application.

## Photo Requirements

## General requirements

- Photo for each visa  applicant must be an un mounted, full face photo as described below.
- Photo must be taken within  the last 6 months, for small children within the last 3 months.
Photo size: Photo submitted must measure  as below mentioned
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head  centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the  top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photo must be a  full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly,  with closed mouth, without smile.
- The face should cover about  70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photo must be in color,  made on matte or glossy paper and have only a white background. Photos with  dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In general, the applicant's  head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head  to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It is  preferable that ears be exposed.
- Sunglasses or other wear,  which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical  reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photo depicting a person  wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate  identification is not acceptable.
Attention: Please follow these  instructions carefully. If photos presented do not meet these requirements your  application will be considered incomplete
More Information
 For more information about visa  application photo specifications, visit click here  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4JKELG9sj6WOHYM1Qlkdil/73b6265005fa368665d8014efda27816/photo-specification.Pdf)

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to apply for a visa 1 month in advance to the travel dates (due to the long delivery dates, which are not within the Embassy’s influence and take sometimes more than 1 week for 1 direction). 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Forms
 This page hosts the form required for  your Visa  Application . Please click on the relevant form to download your visa  application form.

If you can’t prepare the Visa  Application form by yourself, our manager can fill  the Visa application form for you.
Note 
 Minorities (before 16) must have 2  signatures in application form from relatives: mother and father.
 Minorities (after 16) must have 3  signatures in application form from relatives: child, mother and father.
Application form filling
 Dear applicants, kindly be informed,  that our consultants of Service Visa Center of Austria can fill in  your visa application form.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Please note:
If you fall under one of the below categories, please contact us via email for appointment booking:
- Business/cultural events and students
- The inviting company in Austria should sends us an e-mail with an enclosed business invitation and the contact details of the applicants (name, DOB, phone number, e-mail etc.). Upon reception of the contact details our operator will contact the applicants and give them an appointment. Students should be able to book appointments by themselves by submitting the invitation of the Austrian University.
- Participants at events from international organizations
- Participants at events organized by International Organizations based in Vienna, should send an e-mail with the according invitation directly.
If your travel to Austria is for one of the following purpose,  you are eligible to apply for this visa.
- Members of official  delegations
- Employees travelling on  business
- Self-employed people  travelling on business
- Drivers (international  cargo)
- Members of train,  refrigerator and locomotive crews for up to 90 days/year
- Journalists
- Freelance journalists
- Workers posted to Germany  for specific short-term activities
- Scientists with  scholarship from public funds or visiting scientists invited by a university or  other public research facility
- Holiday jobs and work  placements
Description 
If you plan to go to Austria to visit  relatives for a period not to exceed 90 days and your country is not on the  visa-free list, you need a Schengen travel visa (Visa C). This visa is not a work permit.
 Please note that in order to apply for a travel visa at the Austrian Embassy,  your only destination or your main destination (longest duration of stay) must  be Austria. In the absence of a main destination (e.g. same duration of stay in  two or more countries) you can apply at any of these countries but it is  recommended that you apply at the country of first entry because your passport  will show the entry stamp of your first destination only.
Main Rules: 
- Submission process can be  strictly realized by personal presence if the applicant must provide biometric  data.
- Austria Service Visa  Center doesn’t accept documents form third persons by letter of attorney or  corporative attorney.
Note 
Before submission kindly check if your dates in Austria are longer than  to other countries of Schengen area.
All documents that are not in English or German must be translated with a certified translation: seal and signature.
Please, check the quality and fullness of the translated documents as any incompliance is the applicant’s responsibility when submitting the documents.
If a family with minors wants to apply, please, book firstly for the adults.
Please be informed that zero balance statements are not of use for the visa processing process.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If your travel to Austria is for one of the following purpose,  you are eligible to apply for this visa.
- Tourists
- Private visitors  (excluding family visits)
- Property owners in the  Schengen area and their close relatives
- Visitors of military and  civil burial grounds
- Medical Treatment
Description 
 If you plan to go to Austria as a tourist for a period not to  exceed 90 days and your country is not on the visa-free list, you need a  Schengen travel visa (Visa C). This visa is not a work permit.
 Please note that in order to apply for a travel visa at the Austrian Embassy,  your only destination or your main destination (longest duration of stay) must  be Austria. In the absence of a main destination (e.g. same duration of stay in  two or more countries) you can apply at any of these countries but it is  recommended that you apply at the country of first entry because your passport  will show the entry stamp of your first destination only.
Main Rules: 
- Submission process can be  strictly realized by personal presence if the applicant must provide biometric  data.
- Austria Service Visa  Center doesn’t accept documents form third persons by letter of attorney or  corporative attorney.
Note 
Before submission kindly check if your dates in Austria are longer than  to other countries of Schengen area.
All documents that are not in English or German must be translated with a certified translation: seal and signature.
Please, check the quality and fullness of the translated documents as any incompliance is the applicant’s responsibility when submitting the documents.
If a family with minors wants to apply, please, book firstly for the adults.
Please be informed that zero balance statements are not of use for the visa processing process.

 Minorities (before 16) must have 2  signatures in application form from relatives: mother and father.
 Minorities (after 16) must have 3  signatures in application form from relatives: child, mother and father.
Application form filling
 Dear applicants, kindly be informed,  that our consultants of Service Visa Center of Austria can fill in  your visa application form.

[Services]

Optional value added services

visa_types.processing_time:
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to apply for a visa 1 month in advance to the travel dates (due to the long delivery dates, which are not within the Embassy’s influence and take sometimes more than 1 week for 1 direction).
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

If you can’t prepare the Visa  Application form by yourself, our manager can fill  the Visa application form for you.
Note 
 Minorities (before 16) must have 2  signatures in application form from relatives: mother and father.
 Minorities (after 16) must have 3  signatures in application form from relatives: child, mother and father.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For the appointment booking for close family members (in ascending and descending line, spouses, registered partners and siblings) please proceed as follows:
The family member in Austria sends an invitation via e-mail to the visa center together with a copy of their passport and, if not Austrian, residence permit, the birth and/or marriage certificates which prove that the applicant is a family member. Alternatively, a copy of the electronical guarantee letter (Elektronische Verplfichtungserklärung/EVE) can be sent.
If your travel to Austria is for one of the following purpose,  you are eligible to apply for this visa.
- Close relatives visiting Armenian  citizens residing legally in Austria
- Close relatives visiting  EU (EEA and Swiss) citizens
Description 
If you plan to go to Austria to visit  relatives for a period not to exceed 90 days and your country is not on the  visa-free list, you need a Schengen travel visa (Visa C). This visa is not a work permit.
 Please note that in order to apply for a travel visa at the Austrian Embassy,  your only destination or your main destination (longest duration of stay) must  be Austria. In the absence of a main destination (e.g. same duration of stay in  two or more countries) you can apply at any of these countries but it is  recommended that you apply at the country of first entry because your passport  will show the entry stamp of your first destination only.
Note 
Before submission kindly check if your dates in Austria are longer than  to other countries of Schengen area.
All documents that are not in English or German must be translated with a certified translation: seal and signature.
Please, check the quality and fullness of the translated documents as any incompliance is the applicant’s responsibility when submitting the documents.
If a family with minors wants to apply, please, book firstly for the adults.
Please be informed that zero balance statements are not of use for the visa processing process.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
For the appointment booking for close family members (in ascending and descending line, spouses, registered partners and siblings) please proceed as follows:
The family member in Austria sends an invitation via e-mail to the visa center together with a copy of their passport and, if not Austrian, residence permit, the birth and/or marriage certificates which prove that the applicant is a family member. Alternatively, a copy of the electronical guarantee letter (Elektronische Verplfichtungserklärung/EVE) can be sent.
If your travel to Austria is for one of the following purpose,  you are eligible to apply for this visa.
- Close relatives visiting Armenian  citizens residing legally in Austria
- Close relatives visiting  EU (EEA and Swiss) citizens
Description 
If you plan to go to Austria to visit  relatives for a period not to exceed 90 days and your country is not on the  visa-free list, you need a Schengen travel visa (Visa C). This visa is not a work permit.
 Please note that in order to apply for a travel visa at the Austrian Embassy,  your only destination or your main destination (longest duration of stay) must  be Austria. In the absence of a main destination (e.g. same duration of stay in  two or more countries) you can apply at any of these countries but it is  recommended that you apply at the country of first entry because your passport  will show the entry stamp of your first destination only.
Note 
Before submission kindly check if your dates in Austria are longer than  to other countries of Schengen area.
All documents that are not in English or German must be translated with a certified translation: seal and signature.
Please, check the quality and fullness of the translated documents as any incompliance is the applicant’s responsibility when submitting the documents.
If a family with minors wants to apply, please, book firstly for the adults.
Please be informed that zero balance statements are not of use for the visa processing process.

## Visa Fees
The rates are as below. They may be varied at any time. Charges are non-refundable, only be paid in cash. Credit cards and other forms of payment are not accepted. Whenever possible the cashier should be presented with the exact money.
The current Consular rate of exchange  will apply.

[Table:  Close Relatives'/family Members' Visits]
Service charge applicable when applying at the Austria Visa Application Centre:

[Services]

Please Note:
- You will be required to pay the VFS service fee online. Please keep your international credit card/debit card handy while booking an appointment.
- Service charges are inclusive of VAT levied per application over and above the visa fees.
Optional value added services
To add value to our customer, we offer  some value added service. You will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the  visa application fee when you attend the visa service centre only. To know  about these services please refer the additional service page of this website.
Note: Please note that you may be charged visa fee even  though you meet the above information. This will be done only in consultation  with the Embassy of Austria.

## Documents Required
Click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3KszJUP7D3uvgQeGrbrS8r/73e539b8fbd95c7fbe339641cd3cab50/visa-by-categories-austria.pdf) to download the list of documents required
Kindly note:
Personal appearance of applicant is required.
All documents must be submitted in original as well as in a clear photocopy at the time of making an application.

## Photo Specifications
 When applying for a visa, you are  required to bring 2 photographs as part of completing and submitting your visa  application.

## Photo Requirements

## General requirements

- Photo for each visa  applicant must be an un mounted, full face photo as described below.
- Photo must be taken within  the last 6 months, for small children within the last 3 months.
Photo size: Photo submitted must measure  as below mentioned
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head  centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the  top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photo must be a  full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly,  with closed mouth, without smile.
- The face should cover about  70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photo must be in color,  made on matte or glossy paper and have only a white background. Photos with  dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In general, the applicant's  head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head  to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It is  preferable that ears be exposed.
- Sunglasses or other wear,  which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical  reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photo depicting a person  wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate  identification is not acceptable.
Attention: Please follow these  instructions carefully. If photos presented do not meet these requirements your  application will be considered incomplete
More Information
 For more information about visa  application photo specifications, visit click here  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4JKELG9sj6WOHYM1Qlkdil/73b6265005fa368665d8014efda27816/photo-specification.Pdf)

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to apply for a visa 1 month in advance to the travel dates (due to the long delivery dates, which are not within the Embassy’s influence and take sometimes more than 1 week for 1 direction).
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Forms
 This page hosts the form required for  your Visa Application (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6ppVa6f1oEzbyxHnArx0nO/61ef94a7969be7c75b0c2a938d1423ec/visa_capplication_form_july.pdf) . Please click on the relevant form to download your visa  application form.
If you can’t prepare the Visa  Application form by yourself, our manager can fill  the Visa application form for you.
Note 
 Minorities (before 16) must have 2  signatures in application form from relatives: mother and father.
 Minorities (after 16) must have 3  signatures in application form from relatives: child, mother and father.
Application form filling
 Dear applicants, kindly be informed,  that our consultants of Service Visa Center of Austria can fill in  your visa application form.

visa_types.name: National D Visa
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you intend to stay in Austria for three to six months (student exchange, short-term study)  and you are neither EU, or EFTA citizen, you require an Austrian national visa (Visa D).
More information can be found on official website of Austrian Embassy in Ethiopia. (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-addis-abeba)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you intend to stay in Austria for three to six months (student exchange, short-term study)  and you are neither EU, or EFTA citizen, you require an Austrian national visa (Visa D).
More information can be found on official website of Austrian Embassy in Ethiopia. (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-addis-abeba)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
An airport transit visa is required from  certain travelers to go through the international transit area of airports,  without actually entering the national territory of the country concerned,  during a stop-over or transfer between two stages of an international flight.  The requirement to have such a visa is an exception to the general right to  transit without a visa through the international transit area.
Main Rules:
- Submission process can be  strictly realized by personal presence if the applicant must provide biometric  data.
- Austria Service Visa  Center doesn’t accept documents form third persons by letter of attorney or  corporative attorney.
Note: 
 Before submission kindly check if your  staying in Austria is longer than in the other countries of Schengen area.
 All documents that are not in English or German must be translated with a certified translation: seal and signature.
 Please, check the quality and fullness of the translated documents as any incompliance is the applicant’s responsibility when submitting the documents.
 If a family with minors wants to apply, please, book firstly for the adults.
Please be informed that zero balance statements are not of use for the visa processing process.
